prompt:
Generate commit messages for the Portal repository.

Use Conventional Commits with concise imperative subjects under 72 characters. Use scopes when they clarify the changed area. Preferred scopes include release, web, docs, deps, update, cli, config, history, transfer, discovery, and progress.

Choose the type by intent: ci for GitHub Actions and automation changes, chore for maintenance and version bumps, docs for documentation-only changes, build for dependency/package/build-system changes, fix for bug fixes, feat for user-facing features, and refactor for internal code restructuring without behavior change.

Format the subject as <type>(<scope>): <description> when scoped, or <type>: <description> when no scope fits.

Include a short body with bullet points only when the change is complex or the reason is not obvious. Do not add generic headings, summaries, tests sections, markdown formatting beyond bullets, or explanations outside the commit message.
